rejected_candidates.title: Migrator can change itself and mint unlimited COVER tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: `COVER.setMigrator()` can only be called by the current migrator contract, and the only exposed path that makes that call is `Migrator.transferMintingRights()`, which is restricted to governance. This is an explicit governance-controlled handoff, not a permissionless mint bug.

rejected_candidates.title: Emergency withdraw loses pending rewards
rejected_candidates.reason: `emergencyWithdraw()` is explicitly documented as `withdraw all without rewards`; forfeiting accrued rewards is the intended tradeoff of the emergency path, not an unintended vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: BitMap index overflow potential in Migrator
rejected_candidates.reason: The bit index is bounded by `_index % 256`, so the shift is always in the safe 0-255 range and does not create an overflow condition.

rejected_candidates.title: Precision loss in reward calculations
rejected_candidates.reason: The code already uses fixed-point scaling via `CAL_MULTIPLIER`; remaining integer truncation only creates minor dust and does not support a realistic exploit or material loss scenario.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing nonReentrant on vesting function
rejected_candidates.reason: `vest()` updates `_vested[msg.sender]` before the external token transfer, so a reentrant callback cannot increase the caller's releasable amount or double-claim.

rejected_candidates.title: Claim function allows any caller to claim on behalf of any address
rejected_candidates.reason: The Merkle leaf is `keccak256(abi.encodePacked(_index, msg.sender, _amount))`, so the proof is bound to the caller's address and cannot be front-run by an arbitrary third party claiming for someone else.

rejected_candidates.title: Weekly total can be set to zero causing reward halt
rejected_candidates.reason: `weeklyTotal` is an intentional governance-controlled emissions parameter. Setting it to zero is an admin decision, not an unintended protocol bug.
